header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Pocos de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Complaints & Compliments -- softening with I'm afraid / There seems to be -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Eating Out -- Complaints & Compliments
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 19 -- Eating Out
phases: Softening: I'm afraid / There seems to be

listenings.text: Good evening, and welcome back. I am your waiter tonight, and I hope you enjoy your meal. Before you start, let me tell you about a small issue in the kitchen. I am afraid we are very busy this evening, so the food may take a little longer than usual. Also, I have to be honest with you: the chef has run out of the fish, so I would suggest the grilled chicken instead. It comes with a fresh salad and a light dressing, and the portion is generous. If anything is not right, please tell me. If a dish is overcooked or undercooked, or if it tastes bland, I will replace it right away. We really want every guest to leave happy and to give us a compliment, not a complaint. So please relax, enjoy the evening, and let me know if you need anything at all. I will be back in a few minutes to take your order.

Hi Tuca, it is Sofia. I am calling because you asked me about that new restaurant downtown, and I have to be honest with you. The first time I went, it was a little disappointing. My steak was overcooked and quite dry, and the soup was bland, with almost no seasoning. I sent the steak back, and to be fair, the waiter was very polite and replaced it quickly. The second time was much better, though. The chicken was crispy and delicious, the portion was huge, and I even took some leftovers home. So my advice is this: go, but do not be shy. If something is overcooked or undercooked, say so politely, and they will fix it. And if the meal is wonderful, give the chef a compliment, because they really appreciate it. Let me know how it goes. Talk soon. Bye.

extra_audio.text: First, Tuca and her friend sat down, and the waiter suggested the grilled chicken, because the kitchen had run out of fish. Then the food arrived, but Tuca's steak was overcooked and the soup tasted bland. After that, she politely told the waiter, saying she was afraid there seemed to be a problem with her dish. Next, the waiter apologized and replaced the steak with a fresh one right away. Finally, the new dish was crispy and delicious, so Tuca gave the chef a compliment and even took the leftovers home.
